contract.goal: Execute the remaining pre-flight gates (V1 enrollment-safety + OQ2 generated-tree-size reconfirm) so W1+ can proceed against verified assumptions. V4/V5/OQ1 are already RESOLVED per PLAN revision 2; W0 captures only the unresolved items as in-diff evidence for the migration PR body.
acceptance_criteria: V1 outcome captured in notes_for_pr: method (empirical OR documented-behavior reasoning with Claude Code docs citation); observation re. whether Claude Code auto-installs/enables an enabledPlugins entry in a checked-in settings.local.json on a colleague's clone; implication for install-scope guidance and README gitignore requirement.

OQ2 reconfirm captured in notes_for_pr: sketched per-plugin generated-tree layout for at least one plugin; approx size in KB; diff-size estimate scaled across the 3 CLI-shipping plugins; cross-check against OQ2 decision (commit generated trees) and R7 footprint.
Checkin written with verdict captured after evaluator pass; notes_for_pr ready for the PR body to reference.
rules_applied: Bundled migration shape (PQ1, re-confirmed in PLAN revision 2): no per-workstream PRs.
Substrate-only unit: panel auto-derivation falls back to evaluator-contract-fit only (per .claude/agents/PANEL-COMPOSITION.md substrate-only fallback).
V1 method is captured-evidence-for-PR-body, not artifact shape; W8 (README rewrite) owns the install-scope decision.
disqualifiers: Edits to tracked files in this working tree (V1 sandbox lives outside; OQ2 sketch is informational and NOT committed as real plugin output).
Premature install-scope commitment (W0 captures, W8 decides).
inputs: projects/2026-05-19-marketplace-portable-install/PLAN.md (§ Verification V1, § Decisions OQ2, § Risks R7)
projects/2026-05-19-marketplace-portable-install/RESEARCH.md (§ F settings.local.json risk framing; § Verified Claude Code plugin API facts lines 198-238)

actions: V1 method selected: documented-behavior reasoning. Rationale: no second-identity machine on hand this session; V7 (Patreon-machine smoke test) is the ground-truth empirical check downstream.

Read Claude Code plugins-reference.md § Plugin installation scopes. Confirmed: --scope local writes to .claude/settings.local.json; --scope project writes to .claude/settings.json with the explicit doc note: 'making the plugin available to everyone who clones the project repository.'

Read Claude Code settings docs. Found one ambiguous quote ('enabledPlugins (Managed settings only) Force-enable plugins from the allowlist regardless of user installation status') in a managed-settings context, NOT a denial that enabledPlugins works at other scopes — the same key is the scope mechanism that --scope project / --scope local write into per the plugin-install doc.

Reasoned by parity: since --scope project's enabledPlugins entry IS honored on a colleague's clone (per doc language), the same key in a checked-in settings.local.json IS also honored at local-settings precedence on whoever loads the repo. Difference: settings.local.json is gitignored *by convention* (per doc), not enforced; the failure mode of an accidental commit is real.
Sized cli/lib (136K, 25 files), cli/verbs/griot (100K, 9 files), cli/verbs/guild (84K, 9 files), cli/verbs/*.ts top-level loom verbs (292K, 30 files), cli/<name>.ts entrypoints (8K each).

Estimated per-plugin generated trees: loom plugin ~436K/55 files (cli/lib + cli/verbs/*.ts + cli/loom.ts); griot plugin ~244K/35 files (cli/lib + cli/verbs/griot + cli/griot.ts); guild plugin ~228K/35 files (cli/lib + cli/verbs/guild + cli/guild.ts). ev / review-skill / agent-loop-full plugins ship no generated CLI tree (skill-only or zero-content).
Aggregate: ~908 KB across 3 plugins, ~125 generated files. Diff-size estimate: roughly 12k-30k added lines on first commit depending on average bytes-per-line.

Cross-checked OQ2 decision (commit generated trees, per PLAN § Decisions): footprint is consistent with the whiteboard substrate-engineer + performance + skeptic panel's 'visible drift > silent publish-time divergence' rationale. R7 (review-time duplication) remains the binding cost and is the explicit responsibility of the PR description per its mitigation.
No tracked files touched in this working tree (V1 was docs reasoning; OQ2 was du/find probes plus arithmetic).

changes_since_previous: First unit on this branch. Plan PR #10 (plan-marketplace-portable-install) merged before this commit; phase-1 execution branch (marketplace-portable-install.migration) cut from updated main per the new <project>.<phase-lazy-name> convention landed in the pre-migration housekeeping commit on this branch (commit 00319b0).
notes_for_pr: V1 outcome (documented-behavior method): Claude Code's --scope local writes enabledPlugins entries to .claude/settings.local.json. The file is gitignored by Claude Code convention but not enforced by the tool. If accidentally committed, a colleague's clone WILL surface the enabledPlugins entry at local-settings precedence — the docs explicitly state --scope project (same key, different file) 'makes the plugin available to everyone who clones the project repository,' and the same key at local scope follows the same hierarchical-settings semantics on whoever loads the repo. The marketplace must be registered on the colleague's machine for resolution to succeed; without registration the entry is dead, but at Patreon where colleagues share repos and may share marketplaces over time, the failure mode is real. Ground-truth empirical check happens in V7 (Patreon-machine smoke test).

V1 implication for install-scope guidance: recommend README primary install command use --scope user (writes to ~/.claude/settings.json, never lands in any repo) for the Patreon 'invisible to colleagues' use case. Document --scope local as a per-project variant — explicitly conditioned on adding .claude/settings.local.json to the consumer repo's .gitignore as a load-bearing mitigation. This is a refinement of PLAN's current --scope local default in the install instructions; W8 (README rewrite) is the workstream that adopts it.

OQ2 generated-tree size estimate: per-plugin trees sum to ~908 KB / ~125 files across loom (~436K/55), griot (~244K/35), guild (~228K/35). ev, review-skill, and agent-loop-full ship no CLI tree. First-commit diff: ~12k-30k added lines (bytes-per-line varies; TS averages 30-80). Consistent with OQ2's 'commit generated trees' decision and R6 ('PR is large') risk; R7 (review-time duplication) is the binding cost and the PR description must explicitly name which files are sync-script output ('generated by scripts/sync-shared.ts, do not review line-by-line; review the script + the source instead'), per R7 mitigation.

Sync-script subset rule clarification surfaced during sizing: the existing cli/verbs/ layout has loom verbs at the top level (cli/verbs/*.ts) and griot+guild verbs in subdirs (cli/verbs/griot/, cli/verbs/guild/). For the sync script to filter cleanly, W7 (sync script) likely either (a) refactors loom verbs into cli/verbs/loom/ for layout symmetry, or (b) hardcodes the loom-verb file list. Decision lives in W7's contract; surfacing here so W7 inherits the context.
